notes:
## New

- Viewed files expand automatically when you click them in the sidebar or jump to them with ⌘P

## New

- Rich markdown diffs — markdown files can be reviewed as a rendered preview with change markers instead of raw text

## Fixed

- The Install 'prologue' Command Line Tool action works again in packaged builds

## New

- Check for Updates… in the Prologue menu

## New

- The welcome screen shows the app version

The first release of Prologue — a macOS app for reviewing git diffs before they leave your machine.

- Review any branch, base, or working-tree diff with virtualized rendering, syntax highlighting, and word-level change highlights
- Leave inline comments and threaded replies anchored to diff lines; threads re-anchor as the code moves and archive with the review
- Generated review guides that walk the diff in a narrative order
- `prologue` CLI (bundled, installable from the app menu) for reading reviews and replying from the terminal or an agent
- Keyboard-first navigation: j/k, n/p, ⌘P file jump, and a ? shortcut cheat sheet
- Auto-updates via GitHub Releases
